english: belonging to others; W.: *zá - nab - čan* (*za nab can*) greedy, ravenous; *nór-nab-čan* (*nor nab can*) greedy of gain or money, covetous.
Lex.; Cs. to draw to, to attract, (Sch. also: 'to remove a thing from its place?'), prob. another form for snúr-ba (snur ba).
to hide, conceal, Lex.
v. rnáṅ-ba (rnang ba).
v. snád-pa (snad pa).
v. snón-pa (snon pa).
v. snóm-pa (snom pa).

1. to extend in length, to lengthen, to pull out, e.g. a piece of India rubber W. - 2. to draw or drag after, to trail, mjúg-ma (mjug ma) Lex. the train of a robe, the tail etc.; fig. to have in its train, to be attended with, nyon-moṅs-bsnár (nyon mongs bsnar) the consequences of sin Sch.
to spin out, to protract Cs.
v. snún-pa (snun pa).
1. the letter p, (tenuis), the French p. - 2. num. figure: 13.

an affix, or so-called article, the same as ba (ba) (q. v.) which, when attached to the roots of verbs, gives them the signification of nouns, or, in other words is the sign of the infinitive and the participle; in the language of common life, however, it is frq. used for the finite tense, and for par (par); affixed to the names of things, it denotes the person that deals with the thing (rtá-pa (rta pa) horseman, čú-pa (chu pa) water-carrier); combined with names of places, it designates the inhabitant (bód-pa (bod pa) inhabitant of Tibet); with numerals, it either forms the ordinal number (gnyis-pa (gnyis pa) the second), or it implies a counting, measuring, containing (bu-mo lo-gnyis-pa (bu mo lo gnyis pa) a girl counting two years, i. e. a girl of two years; kru-gáṅ-pa (khru gang pa) measuring one cubit; súm-ču-pa (sum cu pa) containing thirty viz. letters, like the Tibetan alphabet); frq. it has no particular signification (rkáṅ-pa (rkang pa) etc. etc.), or it serves to distinguish different meanings (rkaṅ (rkang) marrow, rkáṅ-pa (rkang pa) foot) or dialects (Ká-ba (kha ba) B., *Ka* (*kha*) W. snow); pa daṅ (pa dang) with a verb, v. daṅ (dang) 4; in certain expressions it stands, it would seem, incorr. inst. of pai (pa'i): gsó-ba ríg-pa (gso ba rig pa) science of medicine, grúb-pa lus (grub pa lus) structure of the body, dám-pa čos (dam pa chos) holy doctrine (of Buddha).
W. cross, St. Andrew's cross (thus X).
v. pá-til (pa til).
a medicinal herb Wdn.
more corr. པཱ་ཏྲ་, pā-tra (pA tra) (also pa-ṭa (pa Ta) Pth.) Ssk., cup, basin, bowl (esp. for sacrifices); beggar's bowl = lhuṅ-bzed (lhung bzed).
Ssk. = ṭaṅ-ka (Tang ka) Tar. 112, 6: in Bhotan 1 rupee Schr.; in W. (also *pé-ṇa* (*pe Na*)) a copper-coin = Paisa, esp. of foreign coinage.
Hind. पानी, water Lt.
a strip of wood, ledge, border (?) W.
1. the planet Venus. - 2. Friday.
